hero.title: Welcome to GenCite
hero.subtitle: Your commune, simplified. Access municipal services, follow council decisions and participate in local life.

alert.text: E-waste collection — Saturday April 19, 9am-4pm, Community Hall Parking

services.title: Services Directory
services.subtitle: Find all your commune services, online or in person.

transparency.title: Transparency
transparency.subtitle: Your taxes, your data. Discover how your commune uses its resources.

cd-1.title: Central Square Renovation
cd-1.summary: Approval of a CHF 2.4M budget for the complete renovation of Central Square, including new lighting, benches and a fountain.

cd-2.title: 30 km/h Zone Near Schools
cd-2.summary: Extension of the 30 km/h zone around primary schools and the middle school to improve child safety.

cd-3.title: New Municipal Sports Centre
cd-3.summary: Proposal to build a new sports centre with an Olympic-size swimming pool. Estimated budget: CHF 15M.

cd-4.title: Solar Panel Subsidy
cd-4.summary: Subsidy programme for the installation of solar panels on private residential rooftops.

cd-5.title: Parking Privatisation
cd-5.summary: Proposal to delegate the management of municipal car parks to a private company.

report.title: Report an Issue
report.subtitle: Help us improve your commune. Report a problem and track its progress.

form.title: Title
form.titlePlaceholder: Summarize the issue in a few words

report.successMessage: Your report has been submitted successfully!

r-1.title: Pothole on Rue de la Gare
r-1.description: A large pothole has formed in front of number 23, dangerous for cyclists.

r-2.title: Illegal Dumping at Parc des Tilleuls
r-2.description: Bulky waste has been abandoned near the south entrance of the park.

r-3.title: Faulty Lighting on Chemin du Lac
r-3.description: Three streetlights have been out for a week, making the path dangerous at night.

r-4.title: Fallen Tree on Route de Meyrin
r-4.description: A tree fell on the pavement after Tuesday's storm, partially blocking the walkway.

r-5.title: Night-time Construction at Place du Marche
r-5.description: Noisy construction work after 10pm for several nights in a row.

e-1.title: Spring Music Festival
e-1.description: Open-air concert with local and regional artists. Free entry.

e-2.title: Communes 10km Run
e-2.description: Fun run open to all. Online or on-site registration from 8am.

e-3.title: Easter Egg Hunt
e-3.description: Activity for children aged 3 to 10. Snacks provided.

e-4.title: Board Games Afternoon
e-4.description: Intergenerational meetup around board games. Tea and biscuits provided.

e-5.title: Community Assembly
e-5.description: Open assembly for all residents. Agenda: 2026 budget and infrastructure projects.

e-6.title: Local Art Exhibition
e-6.description: Exhibition of local artists. Opening night on May 4 at 6pm.

n-1.title: New Cycle Lane Inaugurated
n-1.summary: The new 2.3 km cycle lane connecting the town centre to the train station was inaugurated this morning, improving cyclist safety.

n-2.title: 2026 Budget Adopted by Wide Majority
n-2.summary: The Municipal Council approved the CHF 40M 2026 budget, up 5% from last year. Education remains the top priority.

n-3.title: E-Waste Collection Day
n-3.summary: A special e-waste collection will take place on Saturday April 19 at the community hall car park, from 9am to 4pm.

n-4.title: Nursery Registration Now Open
n-4.summary: Applications for communal nurseries 2026-2027 are open until May 15. Form available online.

contact.title: Contact & FAQ
contact.subtitle: Find contact information for each department and answers to your questions.

faq-1.question: How do I obtain a proof of residence?
faq-1.answer: Go to the Services > Civil Registry page and fill in the online form. You will receive your certificate by email within 48 hours. You can also visit the counter in person with a valid ID.

faq-2.question: What are the recycling centre opening hours?
faq-2.answer: The recycling centre is open Tuesday to Saturday from 8:00am to 12:00pm and from 1:30pm to 5:30pm. Closed on Sundays, Mondays and public holidays.

faq-3.question: How do I enrol my child in school?
faq-3.answer: Enrolment is done online via the Services > School Enrollment page. You will need a birth certificate, proof of residence and a vaccination record. Deadline: before March 31 for the September term.

faq-4.question: How do I report a problem in my street?
faq-4.answer: Use our Report page to flag any issue (lighting, roads, cleanliness, etc.). You can track the progress of your report in real time.

faq-5.question: Where can I find Municipal Council minutes?
faq-5.answer: All minutes are available on the Transparency page. There you will find council decisions, voting results and detailed budgets.

faq-6.question: How do I book a community hall?
faq-6.answer: Booking requests are made via the online form on the Services > Venue Rental page. Allow at least 2 weeks' notice. Liability insurance is required.

footer.description: GenCite - Your digital commune portal, designed for accessibility, transparency and citizen service.

accessibility.title: Accessibility Statement
accessibility.intro: GenCite is committed to making its digital services accessible to all citizens, in compliance with WCAG 2.1 Level AA guidelines.
accessibility.commitmentText: As a public service portal, we believe that access to municipal information and services is a fundamental right. We continuously work to improve the accessibility of our platform to ensure every citizen, regardless of ability, can use our services.
accessibility.reportText: If you encounter an accessibility issue on our platform, contact us at accessibilite@gencite.ch. We commit to responding within 5 business days.

contrast.title: Contrast & Readability
contrast.description: Minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio on all text. Dark and light modes available.

keyboard.title: Keyboard Navigation
keyboard.description: All interactive elements are keyboard accessible with visible focus indicators.

responsive.title: Responsive Design
responsive.description: Interface adapted to all screens, from mobile to large displays. Zoom up to 200% without loss.

multilingual.title: Multilingual
multilingual.description: Content available in French and English. Lang attribute updated dynamically.

screenReaders.title: Screen Readers
screenReaders.description: Semantic HTML, ARIA landmarks and alt attributes on all images.

reducedMotion.title: Reduced Motion
reducedMotion.description: Respects prefers-reduced-motion preference. Animations automatically disabled.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: GenCite respects your privacy and is committed to protecting your personal data in accordance with the Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection (FADP) and the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

dataCollected.title: Data Collected
dataCollected.content: We only collect data necessary for municipal services: names, addresses, contact details. No data is sold to third parties.

dataSecurity.title: Data Security
dataSecurity.content: Your data is encrypted in transit (TLS 1.3) and at rest. Our servers are hosted in Switzerland, in compliance with the FADP.

yourRights.title: Your Rights
yourRights.content: You have the right to access, rectify and delete your personal data. Contact our DPO at dpo@gencite.ch.

retention.title: Retention
retention.content: Data is retained for the duration necessary for the purposes for which it was collected, then securely deleted.

portalUsage.title: Portal Usage
portalUsage.content: The GenCite portal is made available to citizens and residents of the commune. Usage is free and subject to these terms. Any abusive or fraudulent use is prohibited.

contentOwnership.title: Content & Ownership
contentOwnership.content: The content of this portal is the property of the commune of GenCite. Reproduction is authorized for non-commercial purposes with source attribution.

liability.title: Liability
liability.content: The commune strives to provide accurate and up-to-date information. However, it does not guarantee completeness or accuracy. Official procedures remain subject to verification.

applicableLaw.title: Applicable Law
applicableLaw.content: These terms are governed by Swiss law. Jurisdiction lies with the competent court of the canton in which the commune is located.
